question: Which Python keyword do you use to bring a library or module into your code?
What is a library or module in Python?

options.A: A collection of pre-written tools and code built by other people that you can reuse
options.B: A folder where Python stores old, deleted files
options.C: A book that explains how computers work
options.D: A tool used exclusively to print text on a screen

question: Which command from the random library would you use to pick a random whole number between 1 and 100?
If you have a Python list of items and want to pick one item completely at random, which command should you use?
Look at this line of code: random.randint(1, 10). What is the highest possible number this command could generate?
